hero.sub: An open-source platform to actually understand Spanish law — a knowledge graph that wires every cross-reference together, an AI chat that cites the real BOE, and dashboards that show how the law evolves over time. All on top of the legalize-es corpus.

stats.label: Integrated layers sharing data — API, graph, chat & dashboards
LLM providers supported through MCP tools
Spanish laws indexed from the legalize-es corpus
Open source under the Apache 2.0 license

layersTitle: One platform for the whole legal stack.
layersSub: Every layer ships on the same **FastAPI core** and lives behind a single **typed contract** — so the React app and any external client speak to the **exact same data**.

layers.title: A typed contract over every Spanish law.
layers.body: FastAPI endpoints for laws, articles, versions, diffs, full-text search and stats. The OpenAPI schema is generated automatically and consumed by a strictly-typed React client — no hand-rolled types.
bullets: GET /api/v1/search?q= · full-text across the whole corpus
Pydantic v2 models and an interactive playground at /docs

layers.title: Cross-references, walkable like a graph.
layers.body: A NetworkX graph that links laws, articles and amendments. Walk a node's neighbours, find the shortest path between two norms, surface the most central pieces with PageRank.
bullets: Filterable by topic, status, period or relation type
Ready to swap NetworkX for Neo4j when scaling up

layers.title: A chatbot that actually reads the law.
layers.body: Bring your own model — Ollama, LM Studio, OpenAI, Anthropic or Google. A FastMCP server exposes the API as tools, so answers come back with real citations from the BOE instead of training-data recall.
bullets: Five providers behind a single selector
MCP tools: search law, fetch article, view diff, query graph
Streamed responses with inline source citations
Local-first by default — Ollama runs offline

layers.title: Compliance and analytics at a glance.
layers.body: Plotly dashboards for legislative analysis and compliance tracking: reform pace by year, most-amended laws, sector-scoped alerts.
bullets: Compliance panel · sector-scoped norms and alerts
Analytics panel · reform pace, amendments, trends
Plotly figures served as JSON for any client

howItWorks.title: From Markdown to answer, in one pass.
howItWorks.sub: Each layer rests on the one below it. The parser feeds the graph, the graph grounds the chat, and the API is the single source of truth for every client.

scenes.title: Parser and registry
scenes.body: Markdown from legalize-es, YAML frontmatter, recursive sections. The output is a typed LawRegistry the rest of the stack queries — no reparsing later.

scenes.title: Knowledge graph
scenes.body: Cross-references resolve to BOE identifiers and land as graph edges. PageRank, communities and shortest paths all live on the same NetworkX graph.

scenes.title: Chat with MCP tools
scenes.body: The chosen model (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic, Google) calls search_law, get_neighbors or get_article — and the chat answers with real citations from the corpus.

builtOnTitle: Powered by legalize-es. Shipped as a single binary.
builtOnBody: LexFlow is a thin, intelligent layer over legalize-es — a public Git-versioned repository of Spanish legislation in Markdown. The final deliverable is a standalone binary per OS. No Docker, no Python install — download and run.

builtOnTerminal.text: # Clone, sync the corpus, run the server

stackSub: No experimental frameworks, no proprietary cloud. Every piece is open source, well-documented and easy to swap out without rewriting the rest.
roadmapTitle: A public roadmap, phase by phase.
roadmapSub: LexFlow is being built in the open. The backend is feature-complete; the next milestones live in the React frontend.

roadmap.name: Foundation
roadmap.desc: Repo scaffolding, CI/CD, branch protection, dependabot.

roadmap.name: Core API
roadmap.desc: legalize-es parser, REST endpoints, full-text search.

roadmap.name: Knowledge graph
roadmap.desc: NetworkX model, PageRank, clustering, navigation endpoints.

roadmap.name: Legal chat
roadmap.desc: Five LLM providers, FastMCP tool server, conversation history.

roadmap.name: Dashboards
roadmap.desc: Compliance + analytics Plotly panels with interactive filters.

roadmap.name: React frontend
roadmap.desc: Layout, law explorer, graph canvas, chat UI, dashboards.

authors.title: The people who build it.
authors.sub: LexFlow is the open-source work of two people. If the direction of the project interests you — or you'd like to contribute — their GitHub profiles are where the code lives.

list.name: Víctor
list.bio: AI & Data Intern at NTT DATA Spain. 4th-year Intelligent Systems Engineering at UIE Campus Coruña. Building AI for Formula 1 — from telemetry modelling to multi-agent reasoning.

list.name: Santiago
list.bio: Future engineer passionate about the aviation industry. Building intelligent systems with AI and data to enhance performance and reinforce safety.

ctaTitle: Spin it up in under a minute.
ctaSub: Clone the repo, run `uv sync`, and the full API is live at `localhost:8000` with interactive docs ready to go. If the project direction resonates, drop a star.

footer.tagline: Spanish legislation, alive and connected. An open-source platform built on legalize-es.
footer.legal: Apache 2.0 · pre-alpha · Built in the open · 2026
